title: Dot Blots Analysis in the Separation of Anti-HIV Antibodies
author: Angel A Justiz-Vaillant
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bjm4kk8w
materials: Finally, 5 μL of the substrate 3,3’,5,5’-tetramethylbenzidine (Sigma Chemical Co., St Louis, MO, USA) is added and the mixture is incubated for 20 min.
The membrane is left to dry.

steps.title: Two (2) µl of 1:2 dilutions...
steps.instruction: Two (2) µl of 1:2 dilutions of chicken IgY or 2 µl of serum from rats and cats is dotted onto nitrocellulose paper. This animal specimens tested positive for the presence of anti-HIV antibodies by ELISA, after being immunized with HIV proteins.

steps.title: Place the nitrocellulose membrane in a...
steps.instruction: Place the nitrocellulose membrane in a BioDot SF apparatus (Bio-Rad Laboratories, Richmond, CA, USA).

steps.title: The membrane is blocked with 5...
steps.instruction: The membrane is blocked with 5 μL/well of fetal bovine serum with 1%Tris buffer saline.

steps.title: Then 5 μL of a commercial...
steps.instruction: Then 5 μL of a commercial conjugate (peroxidase-labeled HIV proteins; Murex Diagnostics, Norcross, USA) is added and allow to drain by gravity.

steps.title: The reaction is stopped by washing...
steps.instruction: The reaction is stopped by washing the wells with distilled water under a vacuum.
